description:
Introduce `SpecId(u32)` newtype and `SpecRegistry` struct. Build the registry in `compile_with_backend` with one any-key SpecId per FnIr. Store on CompiledMetadata. No structural conversion of the eight FnId.0-keyed structures yet — that comes in .29.2.1 along with narrow-spec consumption, because those two changes require each other (structural conversion only matters once multiple specs exist; narrow consumption only works once the structures support multiple specs).

This atom is pure infrastructure: types, registry, no behavior change.

## Concretely

- `SpecId(u32)` in src/fz_ir.rs with `Ord` so it's a clean Vec-index key.
- `SpecRegistry` in src/ir_codegen.rs:
  - `keys: Vec<(FnId, Vec<Descr>)>` indexed by SpecId.0.
  - `lookup: HashMap<(FnId, Vec<Descr>), SpecId>` for both directions.
  - `register`, `resolve`, `any_key`, `len`, `iter` accessors.
- Build in `compile_with_backend` after `module_types`: one any-key SpecId per FnIr, with the SpecId.0 == FnId.0 invariant asserted.
- Add `spec_registry: SpecRegistry` to `CompiledMetadata`.

## Acceptance

- SpecRegistry built in the pipeline with one SpecId per fn (any-key).
- `cargo test --release` matrix green.
- CLIF byte-identical to pre-atom baseline (verified by `diff -r`).
- `SpecRegistry` API carries `#[allow(dead_code)]` only on accessors that .29.2.1 hasn't reached yet.

## Blocked by

fz-ul4.29.1.

## Blocks

fz-ul4.29.2.1 (structural conversion + narrow-spec consumption).
title: .29.2: SpecId indirection + per-spec codegen
